summary: Strong China Cat > Rider & Desolation Row, but abrupt Terrapin walk‑off stalls show
blurb: The first set offers solid folk‑rock with a fiery China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider and Jerry’s poignant Desolation Row solo. The second half builds momentum before Jerry abruptly leaves mid‑Terrapin Station, leaving the band to limp through the remainder. Box of Rain closes the night on an uplifting note.
review: April 13, 1986 at Irvine Meadows delivered a show that teetered between brilliance and disappointment. Early in the evening the Dead rolled out tight renditions of New Minglewood Blues, Peggy‑O and Desolation Row, with Jerry’s solo on the Dylan cover drawing particular praise. The highlight undeniably came when China Cat Sunflower segued into I Know You Rider – a burst of energy that still shines despite the later mishap. Unfortunately, midway through Terrapin Station Jerry abruptly walked off stage, an event reviewers describe as sudden and likely health‑related. Brent Mydland tried to keep things moving with synth lines on Peggy‑O and a gritty “Don’t Need Love,” while Bob Weir stepped forward for lead parts, but the momentum was lost. The band recovered enough to finish with a strong Box of Rain encore, offering an emotional payoff that reminds listeners why this era still holds magic despite its flaws.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recording analyses label the sentiment as "mixed," noting both strong moments (China Cat > Rider, Desolation Row solo) and clear drawbacks (Jerry's abrupt Terrapin exit). No consistent positive or negative trend emerges, so no adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with the cleanest upload, decent clarity and captures most of the performance despite minor volume quirks.

key_highlights: China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider sequence shines before disruption.
Jerry’s Desolation Row solo stands out as a poignant moment.
Box of Rain encore delivers emotional closure.
Looks Like Rain and New Minglewood Blues provide bright spots in the first set.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers a moving Desolation Row solo but abruptly walks off mid‑Terrapin Station, halting the show’s momentum.
band_performance.Bob: Steps up for lead parts after Jerry's exit and adds a brief rap in Lovelight, helping keep the band afloat.
band_performance.Brent: Provides solid synth work on Peggy‑O and aggressive keyboards on Don’t Need Love; attempts to drive the set forward but can't fully compensate for the loss of Jerry.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums enter harshly after the walk‑off, struggling to regain flow during Terrapin and the ensuing jam.
band_performance.Billy: Alongside Mickey, fails to smoothly transition through the truncated Terrapin, contributing to a stalled second half.
